Title: Genetic architecture underlying the lignin biosynthesis pathway involves noncoding RNAs and transcription factors for growth and wood properties in Populus

Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential cis-regulatory marker for modifying Ptr-4CL9 expression and fibre width.
Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: LM50 for expression profiling; natural association population
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: eQTN mapping; epistasis analysis; tissue expression correlation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Cis-lncRNA expression significantly correlated with Ptr-4CL9 across tested tissues
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. L17/TCONS_00058839 is a cis-lncRNA associated with Ptr-4CL9 expression. 2. Its allelic variation participates in epistatic control of Ptr-4CL9 expression. 3. It is implicated in fibre width variation through the Ptr-4CL9 regulatory network.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cis-regulatory lncRNA affecting Ptr-4CL9 expression and indirectly fibre width
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as one cis-eQTN locus for Ptr-4CL9 and showed strong negative tissue-expression correlation with Ptr-4CL9
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing xylem and mature xylem / wood-forming tissues
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in tested tissues; correlated with Ptr-4CL9
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: lncRNA RNA-seq SRP073689; CRA000992; genome resequencing CRA000903; eQTN mapping

Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential noncoding marker for regulating Ptr-C4H3 expression and lignin content.
Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: LM50 for expression profiling; natural association population
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: eQTN mapping; epistasis analysis; tissue expression correlation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Showed significant tissue-expression correlation with Ptr-C4H3
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. L51 is a lncRNA regulator in the Ptr-C4H3 expression network. 2. Its genotype interacts with other causal loci affecting Ptr-C4H3 expression and lignin content. 3. It supports the importance of noncoding regulation in lignin pathway control.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Indirectly affects lignin content through regulating Ptr-C4H3 expression
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highlighted in the functional interpretation of Ptr-C4H3
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing xylem and mature xylem / wood-forming tissues
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: lncRNA RNA-seq SRP073689; CRA000992; genome resequencing CRA000903; eQTN mapping

Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential noncoding marker for tuning lignin content via Ptr-C4H3 regulation.
Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: LM50 for expression profiling; natural association population
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: eQTN mapping; epistasis analysis; tissue expression correlation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Showed significant tissue-expression correlation with Ptr-C4H3
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. L70 is a lncRNA participating in the epistatic/eQTN network for Ptr-C4H3. 2. It indirectly contributes to lignin content variation through control of Ptr-C4H3 expression. 3. It illustrates noncoding RNA involvement in wood-property genetics.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Indirectly affects lignin content through regulating Ptr-C4H3 expression
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highlighted in the functional interpretation of Ptr-C4H3
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing xylem and mature xylem / wood-forming tissues
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: lncRNA RNA-seq SRP073689; CRA000992; genome resequencing CRA000903; eQTN mapping

Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary marker in lignin-content regulatory networks.
Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: LM50 for expression profiling; natural association population
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: eQTN mapping; epistasis analysis; tissue expression correlation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: C3H family exhibited low to moderate expression levels in tested tissues
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ptr-C3H2 is a causal locus affecting Ptr-C4H3 expression. 2. It participates in the multi-locus network associated with lignin content. 3. The study highlights cross-talk among lignin biosynthetic genes via eQTN and epistasis analyses.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Indirectly affects lignin content through regulation of Ptr-C4H3 expression
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although enzymatic in nature, in this paper it is highlighted mainly as a causal interacting locus in the Ptr-C4H3 regulatory network rather than as an independently validated focal enzyme.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing xylem and mature xylem / wood-forming tissues
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; genome resequencing CRA000903; eQTN mapping

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in stem tissues; specific tissue pattern for Ptr-4CL9 not fully detailed, analyzed in cambium, developing xylem, mature xylem and leaf
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with fibre width; higher Ptr-4CL9 expression is negatively correlated with fibre width and the gene may affect fibre width partly via peroxidase activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Association mapping; eQTN mapping; epistasis analysis; tissue expression correlation; enzymatic activity association assay (PO activity)
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter context in P. tomentosa association population; constitutive/inducible not reported
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates 4-coumaric acid and related hydroxycinnamic acids to their corresponding CoA thioesters in phenylpropanoid/lignin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (lncRNA/mRNA: SRP073689; CRA000992); genome resequencing: CRA000903; eQTN mapping using RNA-seq of mature xylem; miRNA sequencing: CRA000983; degradome: CRA000989
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: LM50 used for expression profiling; association population of 435 unrelated individuals for genetics
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established in this paper; treated as an important upstream pathway enzyme
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies activated hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA precursors for lignin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Lead SNP Ptr-4CL9_SNP39 associated with fibre width; Ptr-4CL9_SNP34 associated with peroxidase activity
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: LM50 for expression analysis; natural P. tomentosa population for association
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression analyzed in cambium, developing xylem, mature xylem and leaf; correlated with cis-lncRNA in tissue-specific analysis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing xylem and mature xylem / wood-forming tissues
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ptr-4CL9 is a lead lignin-pathway gene associated with fibre width in P. tomentosa. 2. Ptr-4CL9 expression is negatively correlated with fibre width and is controlled by cis- and trans-eQTNs including lncRNA and TF loci. 3. Ptr-4CL9 may influence wood property partly through effects on peroxidase enzymatic activity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker/gene target for improving wood fibre traits through marker-assisted breeding or regulatory engineering of lignin precursor flux.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: C4H family showed low to moderate expression in tested tissues
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with lignin content; Ptr-C4H3 expression is negatively correlated with lignin content and may also alter CAD activity, potentially affecting lignin composition/structure
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Association mapping; eQTN mapping; epistasis analysis; tissue expression correlation; enzymatic activity association assay (CAD activity)
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter context in P. tomentosa association population; constitutive/inducible not reported
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates cinnamic acid to form p-coumaric acid in the phenylpropanoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (SRP073689; CRA000992); genome resequencing CRA000903; miRNA sequencing CRA000983; degradome CRA000989; eQTN mapping by mature xylem RNA-seq
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: LM50 used for expression profiling; association population of 435 unrelated individuals for genetics
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established in this paper; important early pathway enzyme
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies early hydroxylated precursor for lignin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Lead SNP Ptr-C4H3_SNP69 associated with lignin content; Ptr-C4H3_SNP104 associated with CAD activity, suggesting possible effects on lignin composition/structure in addition to lignin amount
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: LM50 for expression analysis; natural P. tomentosa population for association
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Analyzed across tested tissues; expression negatively correlated with lignin content
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing xylem and mature xylem / wood-forming tissues
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ptr-C4H3 is a lead lignin-pathway gene associated with lignin content in P. tomentosa. 2. Multiple causal loci including miRNA, lncRNA and pathway-gene regulators influence Ptr-C4H3 expression. 3. Ptr-C4H3 may affect lignin quantity and potentially lignin composition/structure via association with CAD activity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker/engineering target to modulate lignin content and wood properties in poplar breeding.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a major regulator affecting lignin content, including indirect control through regulation of Ptr-C4H3 expression; previously characterized as a master regulator of lignin content by down-regulating LAC genes
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: miRNA sequencing CRA000983; degradome sequencing CRA000989; genome resequencing CRA000903; eQTN mapping from mature xylem RNA-seq
Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: LM50 for expression profiling; natural P. tomentosa population for association
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Negatively regulates lignin-related genes; reported in this paper/discussion to affect Ptr-C4H3 expression and previously to down-regulate LAC genes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: miRNA expression varied across six tissues
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Post-transcriptional repression via miRNA-guided target cleavage/negative regulation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression profiled across six tissues; involved in lignin-content-associated network
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: LM50 used for expression analysis; 435-individual association population for genetics
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master regulator-like miRNA influencing lignin-content variation in Populus
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Pto-MIR397a is a core regulatory factor in lignin-related wood traits. 2. It participates in the eQTN/epistatic network controlling Ptr-C4H3 expression and lignin content. 3. The paper cites and supports its role as a master regulator of lignin content through repression of lignin-associated genes such as LACs.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing xylem and mature xylem / wood-forming tissues
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising upper-layer regulator for breeding or engineering lignin content because manipulating one regulator may coordinately affect multiple downstream lignin genes.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Association mapping; eQTN mapping; epistasis analysis; degradome sequencing; expression correlation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A SNP in pre-miRNA region (Pto-MIR397a_SNP7) was associated with DBH elsewhere in the study, indicating pleiotropic roles beyond lignin content.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Trans-eQTN regulator contributing to Ptr-4CL9 expression variation and thereby fibre width variation
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; genome resequencing CRA000903; eQTN mapping
Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: LM50 for expression profiling; natural association population for genetics
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Affects expression of Ptr-4CL9 as a trans-eQTN
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: TFs broadly expressed and many strongly correlated with lignin genes; specific pattern for Ptr-HD-ZIP40 not given
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional regulation inferred from TF status and eQTN effects
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Lignin precursor biosynthesis impacting wood property
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Conditionally important regulator in the validated Ptr-4CL9-centered network for fibre width
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ptr-HD-ZIP40 is part of the regulatory network controlling Ptr-4CL9 expression. 2. Its allelic interaction with other eQTNs contributes to variation in Ptr-4CL9 expression. 3. Through Ptr-4CL9, it is implicated in fibre width variation.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing xylem and mature xylem / wood-forming tissues
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulatory marker for tuning lignin-related fibre traits.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: eQTN mapping; epistasis analysis; expression-based network inference
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified specifically in the in-depth functional interpretation of Ptr-4CL9.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Causal TF locus associated with lignin content and regulating expression of Ptr-COMT30 and additional lignin genes, thereby affecting phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; genome resequencing CRA000903; eQTN mapping
Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: LM50 for expression profiling; natural association population for genetics
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Regulates Ptr-COMT30 and additional lignin biosynthetic genes as eQTN locus
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional regulation inferred from TF status and eQTN effects
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important regulator because its polymorphism is both trait-associated and expression-associated for lignin-related genes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ptr-HD-ZIP54 is a causal TF locus associated with lignin content. 2. It acts as an eQTN affecting Ptr-COMT30 and additional lignin-pathway genes. 3. The paper proposes that it contributes to phenotype both directly and through transcriptional regulation of downstream genes.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing xylem and mature xylem / wood-forming tissues
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulatory biomarker for lignin-related wood improvement.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Association mapping; eQTN mapping; expression-phenotype correlation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as a representative causal regulator in the eQTN interpretation section.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes as a trans-eQTN regulator in the network controlling Ptr-LAC27 expression, thereby influencing fibre width
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; genome resequencing CRA000903; eQTN mapping
Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: LM50 for expression profiling; natural association population
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Affects Ptr-LAC27 expression as a trans-eQTN
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional regulation inferred from TF status and eQTN effects
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Supportive regulator in a validated expression network linked to fibre width
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ptr-TCR2 is one of four causal regulators inferred to affect Ptr-LAC27 expression. 2. Through Ptr-LAC27 expression dynamics, it is implicated in fibre width variation. 3. It exemplifies TF participation in lignin-related eQTN networks.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing xylem and mature xylem / wood-forming tissues
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary marker in multilocus breeding models for wood traits.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a lead gene of the paper, but specifically highlighted in the Results as a causal regulator of Ptr-LAC27 expression.
